artifacts.archives.json: verified Zenodo archive inventory including local SHA-256 after download
artifacts.authority.json: resolved copy of the committed authority manifest plus exact tool identity
artifacts.complete.json: completion-last self-hashed root over every required artifact
artifacts.corpus.jsonl: one exact-byte row for every official metadata benchmark
artifacts.decisions.jsonl: one terminal eligibility or selection decision per metadata benchmark
artifacts.historical.jsonl: normalized evidence for each admitted and rejected historical year
artifacts.producer.json: two locked official executions and their equality result
artifacts.selected-files.jsonl: official-selected order with exact size and SHA-256
artifacts.summary.json: per-logic transition counts and artifact digests

canonicalization.json: UTF-8, sorted keys, no insignificant whitespace, one final LF
canonicalization.jsonl: benchmark_id byte order; canonical JSON object plus LF per row
canonicalization.selected_txt: benchmark_id byte order; one ID plus LF per row

invariants.statement: Every authority input matches its pinned URL, size, and SHA-256 before use.
All 90 Zenodo files match record 16740866 name, size, and published MD5 before extraction.
Archive extraction admits only regular files below the fresh registered root.
Official metadata and the extracted corpus form a 450472-row bijection.
Every corpus row records bytes and SHA-256 computed from the extracted file.
Submission expansion derives competitive logic from more than one competitive participation row.
The 38 competitive submissions have integer seeds summing to 9684066285 with none missing.
The one global seed is 22731158 after modulo 2^30 and NYSE addition.
The two explicit removals occur before competitive-logic, difficulty, and cap selection.
A file with any sat/unsat incoherence across 2018-2024 has no admitted historical year.
A historical year is competitive only when it retains more than one result row.
A file is trivial only when it has an admitted year and every admitted year has only decided results at CPU time at most 1.0 seconds.
New means the complete normalized family string starts with 2025.
Each competitive logic uses the pinned 300, 0.5, 1000, and 0.1 cap expression.
Official output selects min(cap,new_count) new rows and fills the remainder with old rows.
The official producer runs twice with Polars 1.39.2 and emits byte-identical normalized outputs.
Every metadata row has exactly one internally consistent terminal decision reason.
Publication occurs only after the self-hashed completion root validates from a fresh process.

mutations.rejects: benchmark or historical data hash drift
submission removal, duplication, or seed drift
Zenodo record, version, archive count, or archive size drift
duplicate metadata or decision benchmark ID
path traversal, absolute path, separator-bearing component, symlink, or non-regular corpus entry
false coherent or competitive historical year
strict-less-than substitution at the inclusive 1.0-second boundary
explicitly removed, noncompetitive, or trivial selected row
wrong per-logic cap or new-before-old quota
unknown or duplicate official selected ID
missing, duplicate, unknown, or inconsistent terminal decision reason
producer and independent auditor membership disagreement
missing dependency artifact, digest drift, or premature completion publication
